questions.question: What is teacher forcing during seq2seq training?
options: Adding a teacher network during inference
Feeding the ground-truth previous token (instead of the model's prediction) as decoder input
questions.explanation: Teacher forcing stabilizes training by using true previous tokens as decoder input; without it, early prediction errors cascade and the model never converges.

questions.question: Why does fixed context-vector seq2seq accuracy fall as input length grows?
options: All information about the source must fit in a single fixed-size encoder hidden state, which loses detail on long inputs
questions.explanation: The fixed context-vector bottleneck means the encoder must compress an entire sequence into one hidden state — lossless compression of 40+ tokens is impossible.

questions.question: What is exposure bias?
options: The train/inference gap from training on ground-truth tokens but generating from the model's own predictions at inference
questions.explanation: The model never practiced recovering from its own mistakes during training (teacher forcing always corrects it), so errors compound at inference — the train/inference distribution mismatch.

questions.question: Why does beam search often outperform greedy decoding for generation?
options: Beam search keeps the top-k partial sequences alive at each step instead of irrevocably committing to one token
questions.explanation: Greedy commits irrevocably per step; beam search explores multiple hypotheses in parallel and picks the highest-scoring complete sequence at the end.

questions.question: Which architectural family replaced RNN seq2seq for general generation tasks?
options: Transformer encoder-decoder models (BART, T5, mBART, NLLB)
questions.explanation: Transformer encoder-decoders dropped recurrence, enabling full parallelism and attention over the entire source at every decoder step — the same encoder-decoder shape, a different mechanism.

questions.question: The lesson's `greedy_decode` function irrevocably picks the highest-probability token each step. Which production failure does this create?
options: Greedy can repeat or loop and cannot backtrack from a locally good but globally poor token choice
questions.explanation: Greedy decoding's irrevocable per-step commitment causes repetition loops and globally poor sequences — use beam search or sampling for user-facing generation.
